evals.name: laravel-course-platform
evals.prompt: I'm building a Laravel 11 + React app for selling online courses. Users pay once to unlock a course. I've got Sanctum auth set up already. I want to integrate Razorpay so users can checkout from the React frontend and the course gets unlocked after payment goes through.
evals.expected_output: Should analyze the Laravel+React+Sanctum stack, ask clarifying business questions, present a 3-phase plan with payment flow diagram, mention signature verification and environment variables, and NOT start writing code without approval.

assertions.name: no-premature-code
assertions.description: Does not write implementation code before getting explicit user approval

assertions.name: stack-detection
assertions.description: Correctly identifies Laravel 11, React, and Sanctum from the prompt

assertions.name: payment-flow-diagram
assertions.description: Presents a visual payment flow diagram (ASCII or structured)

assertions.name: signature-verification
assertions.description: Mentions HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for payment security

assertions.name: env-vars-for-keys
assertions.description: Advises using environment variables for Razorpay keys, not hardcoding

assertions.name: webhook-coverage
assertions.description: Covers webhook handling as part of the integration plan

assertions.name: approval-gate
assertions.description: Explicitly asks for user approval or states it will wait before implementing

evals.name: nextjs-ecommerce-checkout
evals.prompt: My Next.js 14 app uses the App Router and Postgres. It's an e-commerce site for handmade jewelry. Cart and auth are done. I just need to plug in Razorpay for checkout — what's the plan?
evals.expected_output: Should detect Next.js App Router pattern, ask about order flow specifics, present a structured implementation plan using API routes/Server Actions, mention webhook setup, and gate implementation behind user approval.

assertions.name: no-premature-code
assertions.description: Does not write implementation code before getting explicit user approval

assertions.name: stack-detection
assertions.description: Correctly identifies Next.js 14 App Router and Postgres

assertions.name: payment-flow-diagram
assertions.description: Presents a visual payment flow diagram (ASCII or structured)

assertions.name: signature-verification
assertions.description: Mentions HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for payment security

assertions.name: env-vars-for-keys
assertions.description: Advises using environment variables for Razorpay keys, not hardcoding

assertions.name: webhook-coverage
assertions.description: Covers webhook handling as part of the integration plan

assertions.name: approval-gate
assertions.description: Explicitly asks for user approval or states it will wait before implementing

evals.name: express-subscription-saas
evals.prompt: I have a Node.js Express REST API. Users subscribe to monthly plans — ₹499/month basic and ₹999/month pro. I need Razorpay integrated with webhook support so the subscription activates automatically right after payment. Currently no payment setup at all.
evals.expected_output: Should handle subscription-specific flow (not just one-time), ask about plan activation logic, mention recurring payment / subscription API considerations, cover webhook event handling for payment.captured, and present a plan before implementing.

assertions.name: no-premature-code
assertions.description: Does not write implementation code before getting explicit user approval

assertions.name: stack-detection
assertions.description: Correctly identifies Node.js Express and subscription-based billing context

assertions.name: payment-flow-diagram
assertions.description: Presents a visual payment flow diagram (ASCII or structured)

assertions.name: signature-verification
assertions.description: Mentions HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for payment security

assertions.name: env-vars-for-keys
assertions.description: Advises using environment variables for Razorpay keys, not hardcoding

assertions.name: webhook-coverage
assertions.description: Covers webhook handling and subscription activation via payment.captured event

assertions.name: approval-gate
assertions.description: Explicitly asks for user approval or states it will wait before implementing
